name: Create consignee
pageTitle: Add consignee - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD
heading: Add consignee
legacyTemplate: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/traders/economic_operator_create.html — shared trader-create template used for consignee, consignor, importer, transporter etc. The consignee variant is driven by economic-operator type 'consignee'; the H1 'Add consignee' comes from {{concat 'Add ' (toLowercase title)}} (or addressBookType) at economic_operator_create.html:17-21, and the field labels/order match this page's structure exactly (address-line-1..3, city-or-town, postcode, telephone, country select, email at economic_operator_create.html:81-187).

structure.govukComponent: Bespoke non-GDS caption: rendered as a <span class="heading-tertiary"> inside a <div class="govuk-!-padding-top-6"> above (and outside) the <main>, NOT a govuk-caption-* class. The reference/type text is split across two spans ('DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057' and ' - CHEDD'). Confirmed trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60. Legacy: rendered by the {{> common/referenceNumberHeaderDiv}} partial (economic_operator_create.html:9-11).

Link (govuk-link inside a govuk-body paragraph) — acts as the page-level back/cancel navigation to the consignee search. Legacy: rendered only when a searchUrl is present ({{#if searchUrl}} at economic_operator_create.html:28-32).

Label (govuk-label govuk-label--m) — standalone medium label above the first text input. Legacy: label text is (concat (toSentenceCase addressBookType/displayName) ' name'); for the consignee type this resolves to 'Consignee name' (economic_operator_create.html:52-74).
Fieldset with legend (govuk-fieldset__legend govuk-fieldset__legend--m) grouping the address block. Legacy: legend text is {{toSentenceCase displayName}} (economic_operator_create.html:76-79).

govukComponents.usage: Groups the address block under the legend 'Consignee'
Field labels; the 'Consignee name' label carries the --m medium modifier, address-block labels are the default weight
Consignee name, Address lines 1-3, City or town, Postcode or ZIP code, Telephone number, Email address — all half-width
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60; trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 61

govukComponents.usage: 'Return to search' navigation link back to the consignee search page

nonStandardPatterns.description: The notification reference/type caption ('DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD') is rendered by a bespoke <span class="heading-tertiary"> (inside <div class="govuk-!-padding-top-6">) sitting above and OUTSIDE the <main> element — it does not appear in main.innerText. This is a legacy non-GDS class doing the job that govuk-caption-xl should do. The rebuild should replace it with a proper govuk-caption-xl inside the page content.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (before snapshot: span.heading-tertiary contains child spans 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057' + ' - CHEDD'; querySelector('.govuk-caption-xl,.govuk-caption-l,.govuk-caption-m') returns null)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Validation-message labels differ from rendered field labels
nonStandardPatterns.description: Two of this page's error messages address the field by a shorter/different name than the rendered <label>. The postcode error uses 'Post code' (labels/en.js:81) but the rendered field label is 'Postcode or ZIP code'; the telephone error uses 'Telephone' (labels/en.js:97) but the rendered field label is 'Telephone number'. This is a legacy inconsistency between the frontend labels catalogue (used to compose Joi error copy) and the template's hard-coded labelText strings. The rebuild should make error copy and field labels agree.

fields.name: company-name
fields.label: Consignee name
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label/control/value confirmed). autocomplete=organization. Corroborated by page object getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Consignee name' }) at page-objects/notification/ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:7-9 and driven with 'Global Corp' via fillAddress (ched-d-workflows.ts:112, DEFAULT_CONFIG.consigneeAddress.name). LEGACY: mandatory — Joi COMPANY_NAME is .required() at economic_operator.js:26-29, max length 255. The field maps to the 'company-name' input (validation_constants.js:200; template economic_operator_create.html:52-74).

fields.name: address-line-1
fields.label: Address line 1
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 61 (label/control/value confirmed). autocomplete=address-line1. Corroborated by page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:11-13 and driven with '102 High St' via fillAddress (ched-d-workflows.ts:113). LEGACY: mandatory — Joi ADDRESS_LINE_1 is .required() at economic_operator.js:30-33, max length 255.

fields.name: address-line-2
fields.label: Address line 2 (optional)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label explicitly '(optional)'). autocomplete=address-line2. Corroborated by page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:15-17; driven with 'Suite 3' via fillAddress (ched-d-workflows.ts:114). LEGACY: optional — Joi ADDRESS_LINE_2 is .allow('') with no .required() at economic_operator.js:34-36; only a max-length (255) rule applies.

fields.name: address-line-3
fields.label: Address line 3 (optional)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label explicitly '(optional)'). autocomplete=address-line3. Corroborated by page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:19-21; driven with 'Building 7' via fillAddress (ched-d-workflows.ts:115). LEGACY: optional — Joi ADDRESS_LINE_3 is .allow('') with no .required() at economic_operator.js:37-39; only a max-length (255) rule applies.

fields.name: city-or-town
fields.label: City or town
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label/control/value confirmed). autocomplete=address-level2. Corroborated by page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:23-25; driven with 'Riverdale' via fillAddress (ched-d-workflows.ts:116). LEGACY: mandatory — Joi CITY_OR_TOWN is .required() at economic_operator.js:40-43, max length 58 (note: shorter cap than the address lines' 255).

fields.name: postcode
fields.label: Postcode or ZIP code
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label/control confirmed in the DOM, but NOT filled in either evidence trace). autocomplete=postal-code. The value '10001' is inferred, not trace-confirmed: the page object drives this field via fillAddress (ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:27-29) with DEFAULT_CONFIG.consigneeAddress.postcode = '10001' (ched-d-workflows.ts:117) and the accessibility test fills the same (ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:126). LEGACY CORRECTION: postcode is OPTIONAL — Joi POSTCODE is .allow('') with no .required() at economic_operator.js:49-51 (only a max-length 32 rule). This OVERRIDES the earlier inferred required=true (which was a GDS-convention guess from the unmarked label). Mandatoriness is a policy the rebuild may revisit, but per the legacy system a consignee could be saved with no postcode.

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Telephone number
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label/control confirmed, type=tel, autocomplete=tel). Page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:31-33 drives it via fillAddress with '01100100100' (ched-d-workflows.ts:118) — the value is inferred, not trace-confirmed. LEGACY: mandatory — Joi TELEPHONE is .required() at economic_operator.js:52-55, max length 30. This RESOLVES the earlier ambiguity (label not marked optional, HTML required=false): the legacy server-side validator did require telephone on the consignee create form. Note: this is stricter than the model-level ContactDetails rule (email OR telephone) — the per-page form demanded BOTH. Mandatoriness is a policy the rebuild may revisit.

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60. First option is the placeholder 'Please select your country' [selected]; 253 country options follow (full list captured in the a11y snapshot). This is reference data — the new app should source it from a reference-data / countries service, not hardcode it. NOTE: the list mixes UK constituent nations (England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland) in among sovereign states rather than under 'United Kingdom'. The page object selects the country via getByLabel('Country').selectOption(...) (ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:35-37, 55); DEFAULT_CONFIG drives it with country.England (ched-d-workflows.ts:119), and country.England resolves to the option VALUE 'GB-ENG' (types/country.ts:238) — i.e. option values are ISO-3166-2 style codes (GB-ENG for England), not the display text. This corroborates the constituent-nations quirk: England is a first-class selectable option with its own code. LEGACY: mandatory — Joi COUNTRY is .required() and .valid(...countries) at economic_operator.js:56-59; the template default (non-organisation-branch) select uses topOption/placeholder value '' (economic_operator_create.html:166-174).

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60 (label/control confirmed, type=email, autocomplete=email). Page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts:39-41 drives it via fillAddress with 'globalcorp@mail.com' (ched-d-workflows.ts:120) — value inferred, not trace-confirmed. LEGACY: mandatory — Joi EMAIL is .email({ tlds: { allow: false } }).required() at economic_operator.js:44-48, max length 255, and normalised to lowercase (.lowercase()). This RESOLVES the earlier ambiguity: the legacy server-side validator required a valid email on the consignee create form. Note the per-page form demanded BOTH email and telephone, stricter than the model-level ContactDetails 'email OR telephone' rule. Mandatoriness is a policy the rebuild may revisit.

validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:26-29 (COMPANY_NAME .required() → requiredMessage(labels.CONSIGNEE_NAME, 'a')); requiredMessage template `Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}` at messages/en.js:31-33; label 'Consignee name' at labels/en.js:27. Consignee type resolves labelKey CONSIGNEE, article 'a' (economic_operator.js:91-95). Not trace-confirmed — no error state was rendered in the CHED-D corpus.

validationMessages.message: Consignee name must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:26-27 (maxLengthMessage(labels.CONSIGNEE_NAME, 255)); maxLengthMessage template `${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer` at messages/en.js:3-5; label labels/en.js:27.
economic_operator.js:30-33 (ADDRESS_LINE_1 .required() → requiredMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_1, 'an')); label 'Address line 1' at labels/en.js:4; requiredMessage messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: Address line 1 must be 255 characters or fewer
Address line 2 must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:34-36 (ADDRESS_LINE_2 .allow(''), max-length only, maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_2, 255)); label 'Address line 2' at labels/en.js:5. No required message — the field is optional.

validationMessages.message: Address line 3 must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:37-39 (ADDRESS_LINE_3 .allow(''), max-length only, maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS_LINE_3, 255)); label 'Address line 3' at labels/en.js:6. No required message — the field is optional.
economic_operator.js:40-43 (CITY_OR_TOWN .required() → requiredMessage(labels.CITY_OR_TOWN, 'a')); label 'City or town' at labels/en.js:23; requiredMessage messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: City or town must be 58 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:40-41 (maxLengthMessage(labels.CITY_OR_TOWN, 58)); label labels/en.js:23; maxLengthMessage messages/en.js:3-5. Note the 58-char cap (shorter than address lines' 255).

validationMessages.message: Post code must be 32 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:49-51 (POSTCODE .allow(''), max-length only, maxLengthMessage(labels.POSTCODE, 32)); label 'Post code' at labels/en.js:81. No required message — the field is optional. Note the validation label 'Post code' differs from the rendered field label 'Postcode or ZIP code' (see nonStandardPatterns / discrepancies).

economic_operator.js:52-55 (TELEPHONE .required() → requiredMessage(labels.TELEPHONE, 'a')); label 'Telephone' at labels/en.js:97; requiredMessage messages/en.js:31-33. Note the validation label 'Telephone' differs from the rendered field label 'Telephone number'.

validationMessages.message: Telephone must be 30 characters or fewer

validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:44-46 (EMAIL .required() → requiredMessage(labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS, 'an')); label 'Email address' at labels/en.js:46; requiredMessage messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:47 ([STRING_EMAIL]: EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT); EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT constant at messages/en.js:72. Joi email rule .email({ tlds: { allow: false } }) at economic_operator.js:44.

validationMessages.message: Email address must be 255 characters or fewer

validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:56-57 (COUNTRY .required() → requiredMessage(labels.COUNTRY, 'a')); label 'Country' at labels/en.js:34; requiredMessage messages/en.js:31-33.

validationMessages.trigger: value not in the allowed countries list
validationMessages.evidence: economic_operator.js:56-58 (COUNTRY .valid(...countries) → invalidMessage(labels.COUNTRY) on [ANY_VALID]); invalidMessage template `${field} is not valid` at messages/en.js:19-21; label labels/en.js:34.

openQuestions: Field-set completeness is now corroborated on three sides: the trace DOM, the page object ConsigneeCreationPage.ts (exactly these 9 controls, filled unconditionally with no variant branch), AND the legacy template economic_operator_create.html:52-187 (same 9 fields in the same order, plus a conditional 'approval-number' input that only renders for commercial-transporter type — NOT for consignee). No hidden field was missed for the consignee variant.

Required-field status is now RESOLVED from the legacy server-side Joi validator (economic_operator.js) rather than left to GDS-convention inference: mandatory = company-name, address-line-1, city-or-town, telephone, email, country; optional = address-line-2, address-line-3, postcode. Two changes from the trace-only pass: (a) postcode was inferred required but is legacy-OPTIONAL; (b) telephone and email were ambiguous but are legacy-REQUIRED. These are the legacy policy; the rebuild may deliberately revisit any of them (e.g. postcode required for GB addresses, or email/telephone made 'one of' per the ContactDetails model rule).

Per-page vs model-level contact rule: this create form requires BOTH email and telephone individually (economic_operator.js:44-55), which is STRICTER than the model-level ContactDetails 'email OR telephone' rule (ValidationMessages.properties:196-197). The rebuild should decide which rule governs the consignee.

Validation-message labels are inconsistent with rendered field labels for two fields: postcode error copy says 'Post code' (labels/en.js:81) but the field label is 'Postcode or ZIP code'; telephone error copy says 'Telephone' (labels/en.js:97) but the field label is 'Telephone number'. Recorded in discrepancies — a legacy inconsistency the rebuild should not reproduce.

All 16 validation messages above are LEGACY (mined from the frontend Joi validator + message/label catalogues); NONE were confirmed by a rendered trace — the entire CHED-D happy-path corpus submitted valid consignees with zero errors, and the QA repo has no consignee error-message assertions. They are trustworthy for COPY, but a rendered error-state trace (if any exists) would upgrade them to confirmed.

There are no govuk-hint elements on this page — no field carries hint text (confirmed by trace; the template economic_operator_create.html defines no hintText on any input). Confirm no hints are expected in the rebuild.

The section caption 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD' renders via a bespoke <span class='heading-tertiary'> (inside a govuk-!-padding-top-6 div) that sits above and OUTSIDE the <main> element, not via a govuk-caption-* class (see nonStandardPatterns). The rebuild should use a proper govuk-caption-xl inside the page content.

The Country <select> mixes UK constituent nations (England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland) into the sovereign-state list and has no single 'United Kingdom' entry — a reference-data quirk to reconcile in the new app. Legacy renders it via {{> data/countriesSelectHighLow}} (economic_operator_create.html:166-174), i.e. a 'high/low' priority-grouped country partial.

The address fields are grouped inside a single govuk-fieldset with legend 'Consignee' but 'Consignee name' sits OUTSIDE that fieldset with its own govuk-label--m label — confirmed intentional in the legacy template (economic_operator_create.html:52-74 company-name block sits before the <fieldset> opening at line 76). Confirm this grouping is retained for the rebuild.

The consignee form carries four hidden inputs that reveal legacy architecture, not user-facing fields: a CSRF token (name='crumb'), an optimistic-concurrency token (name='etag'), a 'returnUrl' and a 'fromFooterHeader'. Confirmed in the DOM (trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 60) and in the template ('crumb'/'etag' at economic_operator_create.html:25-26). The CDP rebuild will handle CSRF and any concurrency control via its own framework — do not copy these verbatim; they are recorded only as architectural signal.

Legacy also normalises the submitted email to lowercase (Joi .lowercase() at economic_operator.js:44) and caps field lengths (name/address/email 255, city 58, telephone 30, postcode 32). Decide whether the rebuild keeps these caps and the lowercasing.
